findings.evidence: `rg` hits the special cases at `PathSpace/Shift.lean:51` and `:63`, while the arbitrary prefix form is already present but private at `Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:134` and a permutation-only private variant at `Exchangeability/FullyExchangeable.lean:50`.
findings.fix: Expose general lemmas such as `(pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => fun k => x (φ k)) = pathLaw μ (fun k ω => X (φ k) ω)` and its `prefixProj`/`blockLaw` corollary, then derive the shift-iterate forms by taking `φ k = k + r`.
findings.issue: `map_shift_iterate_pathLaw` and `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` expose only the consecutive reindexing `k ↦ k + r`; this duplicates a special case instead of the natural arbitrary-reindex path-law/prefix API.

summary: The new path-law API is specialized to shift iterates even though the natural reusable statement is arbitrary coordinate reindexing, and the needed general lemma already exists only privately.
